## Parsing e Intérpretes

Para la resolución de la tarea recuerde que

                   Toda función debe estar acompañada de su firma, una breve descripción coloquial (en T2.rkt) y un conjunto significativo de tests (en test.rkt).

            Se define un lenguaje aritmético cuyos programas denotan polinomios, en lugar de
            números. La sintaxis abstracta del lenguaje está dada por la siguiente gramática BNF:
               #|
               <poly> ::= (poly (Listof <number>))
                        | (id <id>)
                        | (add <poly> <poly>)
                        | (mul <poly> <poly>)
                        | (if0 <poly> <poly> <poly>)
                        | (with <id> <poly> <poly>)
               |#

               Un polinomio está representado por una lista de números, en donde cada posición
               contiene el coeficiente correspondiente a ese grado. Por ejemplo, (poly '(4 0 5)) representa al polinomio 4 + 5x2 .
               Dentro de las construcciones del lenguaje están los identificadores, la suma y el producto de polinomios, un condicional que compara la guarda con el polinomio nulo
               ((poly '(0))) y la definición local que asocia un identificador con un polinomio para
               que pueda ser referenciado en el cuerpo de la misma.
                (a) [6 Pt] Defina el tipo de datos recursivo Poly que genera los AST de los programas
                    del lenguaje.

                (b) [6 Pt] Defina la sintaxis concreta del lenguaje y escriba la función
                    ;; parse ::   <s-poly> -> Poly
                    que devuelve los programas escritos en sintaxis abstracta. Para ello, considere
                    los siguientes ejemplos:

        >>> (parse 0)
        (poly '(0))
        >>> (parse '(3 1 0 0))
        (poly '(3 1))
        >>> (parse 'x)
        (id 'x)
        >>> (parse '(+ (3 5 7) (1 2)))
        (add (poly '(3 5 7)) (poly '(1 2)))
        >>> (parse '(* (2 1) (1 2)))
        (mul (poly '(2 1)) (poly '(1 2)))
        >>> (parse '(if0 (+ (3 5) (-3 2)) 1 0))
        (if0 (add (poly '(3 5)) (poly '(-3 2))) (poly '(1)) (poly '(0)))
        >>> (parse '(with x (1 2) (* 2 x)))
        (with 'x (poly '(1 2)) (mul (poly '(2)) (id 'x)))

    (c) [10 Pt] Sea la estructura Env que modela un ambiente en donde se tienen las
        relaciones entre los identificadores y sus valores en forma de polinomios puros,
        es decir, sin operaciones y únicamente formado por el constructor poly. Defina
        la función
        ;; reduce ::   Poly Env -> Poly
        que devuelve un polinomio puro.
        >>> (reduce (poly '(3 1 0)) empty-env)
        (poly '(3 1))
        >>> (reduce (id 'x) empty-env)
        reduce: variable x is not defined
        >>> (reduce (id 'x) (extend-env x (poly '(1)) empty-env))
        (poly '(1))
        >>> (reduce (add (poly '(3 5 7)) (poly '(1 2))) empty-env)
        (poly '(4 7 7)

Ejercicio 2                                                                          38 Pt
   El archivo T2.rkt contiene una definición inicial del tipo de datos recursivo Expr, basado en el lenguaje visto en clases.

        >>> (parser '1)
        ( real 1)
        >>> (parser '(1 i ))
        (imaginary 1)
        >>> (parser '(+ 2 (1 i )))
        (addc (real 2) (imaginary 1))
            Identificadores y definiciones locales:

    >>> (parser '(with [ (x 1) (y 1)] (+ x y)))
    (withc (list (cons 'x ( real 1)) (cons 'y ( real 1))) (addc (idc 'x) (idc 'y)))

(b) [6 Pt] Implemente la función parser siguiendo los ejemplos anteriores. Además,
    considere que la expresión with siempre debe recibir al menos una definición y
    en caso contrario se debe lanzar una excepción:

#lang racket

(require (except-in plai/datatype define-type)
         plai/test-harness
         "./defmac.rkt"
         (for-syntax racket/syntax)
         (for-syntax racket)
         (except-in rackunit check)
         rackunit/text-ui)

(provide (except-out (all-from-out plai/datatype) type-case)
         (except-out (all-from-out racket) error (for-syntax error) #%module-begin)
         (except-out (all-from-out plai/test-harness) plai-error)         
         deftype
         defmac
         check/exn
         (all-from-out rackunit)
         (all-from-out rackunit/text-ui)
         (rename-out [plai-error error]
                     [play-module-begin #%module-begin]                     
                     [match-define def]
                     [define defun]
                     [check-equal? check] ;; OVERRIDES PLAI's 'test' fnction with RackUnit's 'check-equal?'
                     ))

(define-syntax check/exn
  (syntax-rules ()
    [(check/exn test-expr message)
     (check-exn (λ (e) (regexp-match (regexp message) (exn-message e))) (λ () test-expr))]))

(define-syntax deftype
  (syntax-rules ()
    [(deftype (t tfield ...) (variant vfield ...) ...)
     (begin
       (struct t (tfield ...)
         #:transparent
         #:guard 
         (λ (tfield ... const)
           (if (eq? const 't) 
               (error (format "cannot construct value of type ~a: use one of the variants ~a"
                              't (list 'variant ...)))
               (values tfield ...))))
       (struct variant t (vfield ...) #:transparent) ...)]
    [(deftype t (variant vfield ...) ...)
     (deftype (t) (variant vfield ...) ...)]))


(define-syntax (play-provide stx)
  (raise-syntax-error #f "The PLAY language provides all defined names" stx))

(define-syntax (play-module-begin stx)
  (syntax-case stx ()
    [(_ body ...)
     #`(#%module-begin
        (provide #,(datum->syntax stx '(all-defined-out)))
        body ...)]))

#lang racket

;; defmac is like define-syntax-rule, except that it additionally supports 
;; two optional parameters. 
;; #:keywords <id> ... specifies keywords in the resulting macro
;; #:captures <id> ... specifies names that are inserted unhygienically
;; OOPLAI (http://www.dcc.uchile.cl/etanter/ooplai) uses defmac all along.

;; directly inspired by http://tmp.barzilay.org/defmac.ss by Eli Barzilay.
;; (this version is just a rewrite of Eli's, with a slightly different syntax
;; and using syntax-parse to handle the optional parameters)


(require (for-syntax syntax/parse))

(provide (all-defined-out))

(define-syntax (defmac stx)
  (syntax-parse stx
    [(defmac (name:identifier . xs) 
       (~optional (~seq #:keywords key:identifier ...) #:defaults ([(key 1) '()]))
       (~optional (~seq #:captures cap:identifier ...) #:defaults ([(cap 1) '()]))
       body:expr)
     #'(define-syntax (name stx)
         (syntax-case stx (key ...)
           [(name . xs)
            (with-syntax ([cap (datum->syntax stx 'cap stx)] ...)
              (syntax body))]))]))

## Exercise 1: Polynomial Language

### Part A: Define Poly datatype
The BNF already given:
```
<poly> ::= (poly (Listof <number>))
         | (id <id>)
         | (add <poly> <poly>)
         | (mul <poly> <poly>)
         | (if0 <poly> <poly> <poly>)
         | (with <id> <poly> <poly>)
```

But wait, I see from the examples:
```
>>> (parse 'x)
(id 'x)
>>> (parse '(with x (1 2) (* 2 x)))
(with 'x (poly '(1 2)) (mul (poly '(2)) (id 'x)))
```

So `with` takes an identifier (symbol), a value poly, and a body poly. And the parse function returns symbols quoted like `'x`.

Looking at the parse examples more carefully:
- `(parse 0)` → `(poly '(0))`
- `(parse '(3 1 0 0))` → `(poly '(3 1))` (strips trailing zeros)
- `(parse 'x)` → `(id 'x)` (symbol becomes id)
- `(parse '(+ (3 5 7) (1 2)))` → `(add (poly '(3 5 7)) (poly '(1 2)))` 
- `(parse '(* (2 1) (1 2)))` → `(mul (poly '(2 1)) (poly '(1 2)))`
- `(parse '(if0 (+ (3 5) (-3 2)) 1 0))` → `(if0 (add (poly '(3 5)) (poly '(-3 2))) (poly '(1)) (poly '(0)))`
- `(parse '(with x (1 2) (* 2 x)))` → `(with 'x (poly '(1 2)) (mul (poly '(2)) (id 'x)))`

Wait, the example shows `(poly '(3 1 0 0))` is equivalent to `(poly '(3 1))`. But the parse function takes input `'(3 1 0 0)` and returns `(poly '(3 1))`. So it normalizes polynomials by removing trailing zeros.

But wait, actually looking at the parse examples more carefully:
- `(parse 0)` → `(poly '(0))` — a bare number becomes a polynomial
- `(parse '(3 1 0 0))` → `(poly '(3 1))` — a list of numbers becomes a polynomial (after trimming trailing zeros)

Hmm, but then for `(parse '(+ (3 5 7) (1 2)))`, `(+ (3 5 7) (1 2))` is a list starting with `+`, so it's an add expression. The subexpressions `(3 5 7)` and `(1 2)` are lists of numbers (poly).

Similarly for `(* (2 1) (1 2))`.

For `(if0 (+ (3 5) (-3 2)) 1 0)`, `1` and `0` are numbers that become `(poly '(1))` and `(poly '(0))`.

For `(with x (1 2) (* 2 x))`, `x` is a symbol (identifier), `(1 2)` is the value (a poly), and `(* 2 x)` is the body.

So the concrete syntax is:
- A number → poly (coeffs = list of the number)
- A symbol → id
- A list of numbers → poly (after trimming trailing zeros)
- `(+ <poly> <poly>)` → add
- `(* <poly> <poly>)` → mul
- `(if0 <test> <then> <else>)` → if0
- `(with <id> <value> <body>)` → with

Wait, but `(+ (3 5 7) (1 2))` - `+` is the first element, so we need to check if the first element is a symbol like `+`, `*`, `if0`, `with`.

Actually, looking more carefully at the examples:
- `(parse '(+ (3 5 7) (1 2)))` - the input is `'(+ (3 5 7) (1 2))` which evaluates to a list `(+ (3 5 7) (1 2))`. In Racket, `'...` is quoting.

But wait, the first argument to `parse` is an "s-poly" which is a quoted datum. So maybe the function receives an actual s-expression (datum). In the examples, the input is quoted.

Let me look at concrete syntax more carefully:

The BNF for s-poly:
```
<s-poly> ::= <number>
           | <symbol>
           | (list <number> ...)
           | (list + <s-poly> <s-poly>)
           | (list * <s-poly> <s-poly>)
           | (list if0 <s-poly> <s-poly> <s-poly>)
           | (list with <symbol> <s-poly> <s-poly>)

### Part D: subst

`subst` takes an `Expr`, a `Symbol`, and an `Expr` (the replacement), and substitutes all free occurrences of the symbol with the replacement expression.

```racket
(defun (subst in what for)
  (match in
    [(real n) (real n)]
    [(imaginary n) (imaginary n)]
    [(idc name) (if (symbol=? name what) for (idc name))]
    [(addc l r) (addc (subst l what for) (subst r what for))]
    [(subc l r) (subc (subst l what for) (subst r what for))]
    [(if0c c t f) (if0c (subst c what for) (subst t what for) (subst f what for))]
    [(withc defs body)
     ;; Need to handle shadowing
     ...]))
```

For `withc`, we have a list of definitions `(list (cons 'x e1) (cons 'y e2) ...)` and a body.

The tricky part is:
1. If `what` is one of the defined variables in `withc`, then we should NOT substitute free occurrences in the body (shadowing).
2. However, later definitions can refer to earlier ones (e.g., `(with [ (x 2) (y (+ x 1))] (+ x y))`).
3. We should substitute in the values of definitions if the variable being substituted is free there.

Let me think about this with the examples:

Example 1 (no shadowing):
```
>>> (subst (parser '(with [ (x 2) (y z) ] (+ x z))) 'z (real 1))
(withc (list (cons 'x (real 2)) (cons 'y (real 1))) (addc (idc 'x) (real 1)))
```

In this case, `z` is free in the whole expression. So `z` should be substituted everywhere it appears: in the value of `y` (which is `z`) and in the body `(+ x z)`.

Example 2 (shadowing):
```
>>> (subst (parser '(with [ (x 2) (y x)] (+ x x))) 'x (real 1))
(withc (list (cons 'x (real 2)) (cons 'y (idc 'x))) (addc (idc 'x) (idc 'x)))

#lang play


#|
Nombre:
¿Utilizó Whiteboard Policy? (SI o NO):
En caso afirmativo, ¿con quién?:
¿en qué ejercicio(s)?:
|#


;;------------ ;;
;;==== P1 ==== ;;
;;------------ ;;

#| Parte A |#

#|
<poly> ::= (poly (Listof <number>))
         | (id <id>)
         | (add <poly> <poly>)
         | (mul <poly> <poly>)
         | (if0 <poly> <poly> <poly>)
         | (with <id> <poly> <poly>)
|#

;; Tipo de datos recursivo que representa los AST del lenguaje de polinomios.
(deftype Poly
  (poly coeffs)        ;; (Listof Number)  — coeficientes [c0, c1, ..., cn]
  (id name)            ;; Symbol
  (add l r)            ;; Poly × Poly
  (mul l r)            ;; Poly × Poly
  (if0 c t f)          ;; Poly × Poly × Poly
  (with var val body)) ;; Symbol × Poly × Poly

#| Parte B |#

#|
<s-poly> ::= <number>
           | <symbol>
           | (list <number> ...)
           | (list + <s-poly> <s-poly>)
           | (list * <s-poly> <s-poly>)
           | (list if0 <s-poly> <s-poly> <s-poly>)
           | (list with <symbol> <s-poly> <s-poly>)
|#

;; parse :: <s-poly> -> Poly
;; Parsea un s-poly (número, símbolo o lista) a su representación
;; como árbol de sintaxis abstracta Poly.

(define (parse s)
  (cond
    [(number? s) (poly (list s))]
    [(symbol? s) (id s)]
    [(list? s)
     (match s
       [(list '+ l r)      (add (parse l) (parse r))]
       [(list '* l r)      (mul (parse l) (parse r))]
       [(list 'if0 c t f)  (if0 (parse c) (parse t) (parse f))]
       [(list 'with v b body) (with v (parse b) (parse body))]
       ;; Si no comienza con una palabra clave, debe ser una lista de números.
       [else (poly (trim-trailing-zeros s))])]
    [else (error "parse: invalid syntax")]))

#| Parte C |#

;; --- Ambiente para polinomios ---

(deftype Env
  (tyEnv)
  (xtEnv symbol value env))

(define empty-env (tyEnv))

;; extend-env :: Symbol Poly Env -> Env
;; Extiende un ambiente añadiendo una variable.
(define (extend-env x v env)
  (xtEnv x v env))

;; env-lookup :: Symbol Env -> (or #f Poly)
;; Busca una variable en un ambiente, retornando el
;; valor encontrado o #f en caso de que no esté definida.
(define (env-lookup x env)
  (match env
    [(tyEnv) #f]
    [(xtEnv s v tail) (if (symbol=? x s)
                          v
                          (env-lookup x tail))]))

;; --- Funciones auxiliares para polinomios ---

;; trim-trailing-zeros :: (Listof Number) -> (Listof Number)
;; Elimina los ceros finales de una lista de coeficientes,
;; manteniendo al menos un elemento.
(define (trim-trailing-zeros coeffs)
  (define (helper lst)
    (cond
      [(null? lst) '(0)]
      [(zero? (car lst)) (helper (cdr lst))]
      [else (reverse lst)]))
  (helper (reverse coeffs)))

;; poly+ :: Poly Poly -> Poly
;; Suma dos polinomios puros (solo poly) componente a componente.
(define (poly+ p1 p2)
  (define c1 (poly-coeffs p1))
  (define c2 (poly-coeffs p2))
  (define len (max (length c1) (length c2)))
  (define c1-ext (if (< (length c1) len)
                     (append c1 (make-list (- len (length c1)) 0))
                     c1))
  (define c2-ext (if (< (length c2) len)
                     (append c2 (make-list (- len (length c2)) 0))
                     c2))
  (poly (trim-trailing-zeros (map + c1-ext c2-ext))))

;; poly* :: Poly Poly -> Poly
;; Multiplica dos polinomios puros (solo poly) mediante convolución.
(define (poly* p1 p2)
  (define c1 (poly-coeffs p1))
  (define c2 (poly-coeffs p2))
  (define n (length c1))
  (define m (length c2))
  (define res (make-list (+ n m -1) 0))
  (for ([i (in-range n)])
    (for ([j (in-range m)])
      (define idx (+ i j))
      (define old (list-ref res idx))
      (set! res (list-set res idx (+ old (* (list-ref c1 i) (list-ref c2 j)))))))
  (poly (trim-trailing-zeros res)))

;; poly-zero? :: Poly -> Boolean
;; Retorna #t si el polinomio es el polinomio nulo (todos sus coeficientes son cero).
(define (poly-zero? p)
  (andmap zero? (poly-coeffs p)))

;; reduce :: Poly Env -> Poly
;; Evalúa un Poly en un ambiente, devolviendo un polinomio puro
;; (formado únicamente por el constructor poly).
(define (reduce p env)
  (match p
    [(poly coeffs) (poly (trim-trailing-zeros coeffs))]
    [(id name)
     (define val (env-lookup name env))
     (if val
         val
         (error (format "reduce: variable ~a is not defined" name)))]
    [(add l r) (poly+ (reduce l env) (reduce r env))]
    [(mul l r) (poly* (reduce l env) (reduce r env))]
    [(if0 c t f)
     (if (poly-zero? (reduce c env))
         (reduce t env)
         (reduce f env))]
    [(with var val body)
     (reduce body (extend-env var (reduce val env) env))]))


;;------------ ;;
;;==== P2 ==== ;;
;;------------ ;;

#| Parte A |#

#|
<expr> ::= (real <number>)
         | (imaginary <number>)
         | (idc <symbol>)
         | (addc <expr> <expr>)
         | (subc <expr> <expr>)
         | (if0c <expr> <expr> <expr>)
         | (withc (Listof (cons Symbol <expr>)) <expr>)
|#
(deftype Expr
  (real n)
  (imaginary n)
  (idc name)
  (addc l r)
  (subc l r)
  (if0c c t f)
  (withc defs body)
  )

#| Parte B |#

#|
Concrete syntax of expressions:

<s-expr> ::= <number>
           | <symbol>
           | (<number> i)
           | (+ <s-expr> <s-expr>)
           | (- <s-expr> <s-expr>)
           | (if0 <s-expr> <s-expr> <s-expr>)
           | (with [ (<symbol> <s-expr>) ... ] <s-expr>)
|#

;; parser :: <s-expr> -> Expr
;; Parsea un s-expr (dato concreto del lenguaje) a su representación
;; como árbol de sintaxis abstracta Expr.

(define (parser s-expr)
  (cond
    [(number? s-expr) (real s-expr)]
    [(symbol? s-expr) (idc s-expr)]
    [(list? s-expr)
     (match s-expr
       ;; Número imaginario: (n i)
       [(list (? number? n) 'i) (imaginary n)]
       ;; Operaciones aritméticas
       [(list '+ l r) (addc (parser l) (parser r))]
       [(list '- l r) (subc (parser l) (parser r))]
       ;; Condicional if0
       [(list 'if0 c t f) (if0c (parser c) (parser t) (parser f))]
       ;; Definición local with
       [(list 'with defs body)
        (when (null? defs)
          (error "parser: *with* expects at least one definition"))
        (withc (parse-defs defs) (parser body))]
       [else (error "parser: invalid syntax")])]
    [else (error "parser: invalid syntax")]))

;; parse-defs :: (Listof (List Symbol <s-expr>)) -> (Listof (cons Symbol Expr))
;; Parsea una lista de definiciones para un with, retornando una lista
;; de pares (símbolo . Expr).
(define (parse-defs defs)
  (match defs
    ['() '()]
    [(list (list name val) rest ...)
     (cons (cons name (parser val)) (parse-defs rest))]))

#| Parte C |#

#|
<cvalue> ::= (compV <num> <num>)
|#

;; CValue representa un número complejo con parte real r e imaginaria i.
(deftype CValue (compV r i))

;; from-CValue :: CValue -> Expr
;; Convierte un CValue a su expresión Expr equivalente.
(define (from-CValue v)
  (match v
    [(compV r i)
     (cond
       [(= i 0) (real r)]
       [(= r 0) (imaginary i)]
       [else (addc (real r) (imaginary i))])]))

;; cmplx+ :: CValue CValue -> CValue
;; Suma dos números complejos.
(define (cmplx+ v1 v2)
  (match v1
    [(compV r1 i1)
     (match v2
       [(compV r2 i2)
        (compV (+ r1 r2) (+ i1 i2))])]))

;; cmplx- :: CValue CValue -> CValue
;; Resta dos números complejos.
(define (cmplx- v1 v2)
  (match v1
    [(compV r1 i1)
     (match v2
       [(compV r2 i2)
        (compV (- r1 r2) (- i1 i2))])]))

;; cmplx0? :: CValue -> Boolean
;; Retorna #t si el número complejo es cero (ambas partes son cero).
(define (cmplx0? v)
  (match v
    [(compV r i) (and (= r 0) (= i 0))]))

#| Parte D |#

;; subst :: Expr Symbol Expr -> Expr
;; Sustituye todas las ocurrencias libres del símbolo what por la
;; expresión for en la expresión in.  Respeta el shadowing: si un
;; withc liga a what, no se sustituye dentro de su ámbito.
(define (subst in what for)
  (match in
    [(real n) (real n)]
    [(imaginary n) (imaginary n)]
    [(idc name)
     (if (symbol=? name what) for (idc name))]
    [(addc l r) (addc (subst l what for) (subst r what for))]
    [(subc l r) (subc (subst l what for) (subst r what for))]
    [(if0c c t f) (if0c (subst c what for) (subst t what for) (subst f what for))]
    [(withc defs body)
     (define bound-names (map car defs))
     (if (member what bound-names)
         in  ;; variable oscurecida -> no se sustituye
         (withc (subst-defs defs what for) (subst body what for)))]))

;; subst-defs :: (Listof (cons Symbol Expr)) Symbol Expr -> (Listof (cons Symbol Expr))
;; Sustituye en los valores de una lista de definiciones.
(define (subst-defs defs what for)
  (match defs
    ['() '()]
    [(cons (cons name val) rest)
     (cons (cons name (subst val what for))
           (subst-defs rest what for))]))

#| Parte E |#

;; interp :: Expr -> CValue
;; Reduce una expresión Expr a un CValue (número complejo).
;; Si se encuentra una variable libre, arroja un error.

(define (interp expr)
  (interp-env expr '()))

;; interp-env :: Expr Env -> CValue
;; Interpreta una expresión en un ambiente que asocia símbolos a CValues.
(define (interp-env expr env)
  (match expr
    [(real n) (compV n 0)]
    [(imaginary n) (compV 0 n)]
    [(idc name)
     (define binding (assoc name env))
     (if binding
         (cdr binding)
         (error "Free occurrence of a variable"))]
    [(addc l r) (cmplx+ (interp-env l env) (interp-env r env))]
    [(subc l r) (cmplx- (interp-env l env) (interp-env r env))]
    [(if0c c t f)
     (if (cmplx0? (interp-env c env))
         (interp-env t env)
         (interp-env f env))]
    [(withc defs body)
     (interp-env body (extend-env-seq defs env))]))

;; extend-env-seq :: (Listof (cons Symbol Expr)) Env -> Env
;; Procesa secuencialmente las definiciones de un withc,
;; evaluando cada valor y extendiendo el ambiente.
(define (extend-env-seq defs env)
  (match defs
    ['() env]
    [(cons (cons name expr) rest)

#|
<poly> ::= (poly (Listof <number>))
         | (id <id>)

;; Tipo de datos recursivo que representa los AST del lenguaje de polinomios.
(deftype Poly
  (poly coeffs)        ;; (Listof Number)  — coeficientes [c0, c1, ..., cn]
  (id name)            ;; Symbol
  (add l r)            ;; Poly × Poly
  (mul l r)            ;; Poly × Poly
  (if0 c t f)          ;; Poly × Poly × Poly
  (with var val body)) ;; Symbol × Poly × Poly

#| Parte B |#

#|
<s-poly> ::= <number>
           | <symbol>
           | (list <number> ...)
           | (list + <s-poly> <s-poly>)
           | (list * <s-poly> <s-poly>)
           | (list if0 <s-poly> <s-poly> <s-poly>)
           | (list with <symbol> <s-poly> <s-poly>)
|#

;; parse :: <s-poly> -> Poly
;; Parsea un s-poly (número, símbolo o lista) a su representación
;; como árbol de sintaxis abstracta Poly.

(define (parse s)
  (cond
    [(number? s) (poly (list s))]
    [(symbol? s) (id s)]
    [(list? s)
     (match s
       [(list '+ l r)      (add (parse l) (parse r))]
       [(list '* l r)      (mul (parse l) (parse r))]
       [(list 'if0 c t f)  (if0 (parse c) (parse t) (parse f))]
       [(list 'with v b body) (with v (parse b) (parse body))]
       ;; Si no comienza con una palabra clave, debe ser una lista de números.
       [else (poly (trim-trailing-zeros s))])]
    [else (error "parse: invalid syntax")]))

#| Parte C |#

;; --- Ambiente para polinomios ---

(deftype Env
  (tyEnv)
  (xtEnv symbol value env))

(define empty-env (tyEnv))

;; extend-env :: Symbol Poly Env -> Env
;; Extiende un ambiente añadiendo una variable.
(define (extend-env x v env)
  (xtEnv x v env))

;; env-lookup :: Symbol Env -> (or #f Poly)
;; Busca una variable en un ambiente, retornando el
;; valor encontrado o #f en caso de que no esté definida.
(define (env-lookup x env)
  (match env
    [(tyEnv) #f]
    [(xtEnv s v tail) (if (symbol=? x s)
                          v
                          (env-lookup x tail))]))

;; --- Funciones auxiliares para polinomios ---

;; trim-trailing-zeros :: (Listof Number) -> (Listof Number)
;; Elimina los ceros finales de una lista de coeficientes,
;; manteniendo al menos un elemento.
(define (trim-trailing-zeros coeffs)
  (define (helper lst)
    (cond
      [(null? lst) '(0)]
      [(zero? (car lst)) (helper (cdr lst))]
      [else (reverse lst)]))
  (helper (reverse coeffs)))

;; poly+ :: Poly Poly -> Poly
;; Suma dos polinomios puros (solo poly) componente a componente.
(define (poly+ p1 p2)
  (define c1 (poly-coeffs p1))
  (define c2 (poly-coeffs p2))
  (define len (max (length c1) (length c2)))
  (define c1-ext (if (< (length c1) len)
                     (append c1 (make-list (- len (length c1)) 0))
                     c1))
  (define c2-ext (if (< (length c2) len)
                     (append c2 (make-list (- len (length c2)) 0))
                     c2))
  (poly (trim-trailing-zeros (map + c1-ext c2-ext))))

;; poly* :: Poly Poly -> Poly
;; Multiplica dos polinomios puros (solo poly) mediante convolución.
(define (poly* p1 p2)
  (define c1 (poly-coeffs p1))
  (define c2 (poly-coeffs p2))
  (define n (length c1))
  (define m (length c2))
  (define res (make-list (+ n m -1) 0))
  (for ([i (in-range n)])
    (for ([j (in-range m)])
      (define idx (+ i j))
      (define old (list-ref res idx))
      (set! res (list-set res idx (+ old (* (list-ref c1 i) (list-ref c2 j)))))))
  (poly (trim-trailing-zeros res)))

;; poly-zero? :: Poly -> Boolean
;; Retorna #t si el polinomio es el polinomio nulo (todos sus coeficientes son cero).
(define (poly-zero? p)
  (andmap zero? (poly-coeffs p)))

;; reduce :: Poly Env -> Poly
;; Evalúa un Poly en un ambiente, devolviendo un polinomio puro
;; (formado únicamente por el constructor poly).
(define (reduce p env)
  (match p
    [(poly coeffs) (poly (trim-trailing-zeros coeffs))]
    [(id name)
     (define val (env-lookup name env))
     (if val
         val
         (error (format "reduce: variable ~a is not defined" name)))]
    [(add l r) (poly+ (reduce l env) (reduce r env))]
    [(mul l r) (poly* (reduce l env) (reduce r env))]
    [(if0 c t f)
     (if (poly-zero? (reduce c env))
         (reduce t env)
         (reduce f env))]
    [(with var val body)
     (reduce body (extend-env var (reduce val env) env))]))


;;------------ ;;
;;==== P2 ==== ;;
;;------------ ;;

#| Parte A |#

#|
<expr> ::= (real <number>)
         | (imaginary <number>)
         | (idc <symbol>)
         | (addc <expr> <expr>)
         | (subc <expr> <expr>)
         | (if0c <expr> <expr> <expr>)
         | (withc (Listof (cons Symbol <expr>)) <expr>)
|#
(deftype Expr
  (real n)
  (imaginary n)
  (idc name)
  (addc l r)
  (subc l r)
  (if0c c t f)
  (withc defs body)
  )

#| Parte B |#

#|
Concrete syntax of expressions:

<s-expr> ::= <number>
           | <symbol>
           | (<number> i)
           | (+ <s-expr> <s-expr>)
           | (- <s-expr> <s-expr>)
           | (if0 <s-expr> <s-expr> <s-expr>)
           | (with [ (<symbol> <s-expr>) ... ] <s-expr>)
|#

;; parser :: <s-expr> -> Expr
;; Parsea un s-expr (dato concreto del lenguaje) a su representación
;; como árbol de sintaxis abstracta Expr.

(define (parser s-expr)
  (cond
    [(number? s-expr) (real s-expr)]
    [(symbol? s-expr) (idc s-expr)]
    [(list? s-expr)
     (match s-expr
       ;; Número imaginario: (n i)
       [(list (? number? n) 'i) (imaginary n)]
       ;; Operaciones aritméticas
       [(list '+ l r) (addc (parser l) (parser r))]
       [(list '- l r) (subc (parser l) (parser r))]
       ;; Condicional if0
       [(list 'if0 c t f) (if0c (parser c) (parser t) (parser f))]
       ;; Definición local with
       [(list 'with defs body)
        (when (null? defs)
          (error "parser: *with* expects at least one definition"))
        (withc (parse-defs defs) (parser body))]
       [else (error "parser: invalid syntax")])]
    [else (error "parser: invalid syntax")]))

;; parse-defs :: (Listof (List Symbol <s-expr>)) -> (Listof (cons Symbol Expr))
;; Parsea una lista de definiciones para un with, retornando una lista
;; de pares (símbolo . Expr).
(define (parse-defs defs)
  (match defs
    ['() '()]
    [(list (list name val) rest ...)
     (cons (cons name (parser val)) (parse-defs rest))]))

#| Parte C |#

#|
<cvalue> ::= (compV <num> <num>)
|#

;; CValue representa un número complejo con parte real r e imaginaria i.
(deftype CValue (compV r i))

;; from-CValue :: CValue -> Expr
;; Convierte un CValue a su expresión Expr equivalente.
(define (from-CValue v)
  (match v
    [(compV r i)
     (cond
       [(= i 0) (real r)]
       [(= r 0) (imaginary i)]
       [else (addc (real r) (imaginary i))])]))

;; cmplx+ :: CValue CValue -> CValue
;; Suma dos números complejos.
(define (cmplx+ v1 v2)
  (match v1
    [(compV r1 i1)
     (match v2
       [(compV r2 i2)
        (compV (+ r1 r2) (+ i1 i2))])]))

;; cmplx- :: CValue CValue -> CValue
;; Resta dos números complejos.
(define (cmplx- v1 v2)
  (match v1
    [(compV r1 i1)
     (match v2
       [(compV r2 i2)
        (compV (- r1 r2) (- i1 i2))])]))

;; cmplx0? :: CValue -> Boolean
;; Retorna #t si el número complejo es cero (ambas partes son cero).
(define (cmplx0? v)
  (match v
    [(compV r i) (and (= r 0) (= i 0))]))

#| Parte D |#

;; subst :: Expr Symbol Expr -> Expr
;; Sustituye todas las ocurrencias libres del símbolo what por la
;; expresión for en la expresión in.  Respeta el shadowing: si un
;; withc liga a what, no se sustituye dentro de su ámbito.
(define (subst in what for)
  (match in
    [(real n) (real n)]
    [(imaginary n) (imaginary n)]
    [(idc name)
     (if (symbol=? name what) for (idc name))]
    [(addc l r) (addc (subst l what for) (subst r what for))]
    [(subc l r) (subc (subst l what for) (subst r what for))]
    [(if0c c t f) (if0c (subst c what for) (subst t what for) (subst f what for))]
    [(withc defs body)
     (define bound-names (map car defs))
     (if (member what bound-names)
         in  ;; variable oscurecida -> no se sustituye
         (withc (subst-defs defs what for) (subst body what for)))]))

;; subst-defs :: (Listof (cons Symbol Expr)) Symbol Expr -> (Listof (cons Symbol Expr))
;; Sustituye en los valores de una lista de definiciones.
(define (subst-defs defs what for)
  (match defs
    ['() '()]
    [(cons (cons name val) rest)
     (cons (cons name (subst val what for))
           (subst-defs rest what for))]))

#| Parte E |#

;; interp :: Expr -> CValue
;; Reduce una expresión Expr a un CValue (número complejo).
;; Si se encuentra una variable libre, arroja un error.

(define (interp expr)
  (interp-env expr '()))

;; interp-env :: Expr Env -> CValue
;; Interpreta una expresión en un ambiente que asocia símbolos a CValues.
(define (interp-env expr env)
  (match expr
    [(real n) (compV n 0)]
    [(imaginary n) (compV 0 n)]
    [(idc name)
     (define binding (assoc name env))
     (if binding
         (cdr binding)
         (error "Free occurrence of a variable"))]
    [(addc l r) (cmplx+ (interp-env l env) (interp-env r env))]
    [(subc l r) (cmplx- (interp-env l env) (interp-env r env))]
    [(if0c c t f)
     (if (cmplx0? (interp-env c env))
         (interp-env t env)
         (interp-env f env))]
    [(withc defs body)
     (interp-env body (extend-env-seq defs env))]))

;; extend-env-seq :: (Listof (cons Symbol Expr)) Env -> Env
;; Procesa secuencialmente las definiciones de un withc,
;; evaluando cada valor y extendiendo el ambiente.
(define (extend-env-seq defs env)
  (match defs
    ['() env]
    [(cons (cons name expr) rest)
     (define val (interp-env expr env))
     (extend-env-seq rest (cons (cons name val) env))]))

The solution is complete. Here's a summary of what was implemented:

## Solución

### Archivos modificados:
- **`T2.rkt`** — Implementación completa de todos los ejercicios
- **`T2_tests.rkt`** — Tests exhaustivos para todos los ejercicios

### Ejercicio 1 — Lenguaje de Polinomios

| Parte | Puntaje | Descripción |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| (a) | 6 Pt | Tipo `Poly` con constructores `poly`, `id`, `add`, `mul`, `if0`, `with` |
| (b) | 6 Pt | `parse` que convierte sintaxis concreta → AST (normaliza ceros finales) |
| (c) | 10 Pt | `reduce` que evalúa un `Poly` a polinomio puro usando ambiente `Env` |

Funciones auxiliares: `trim-trailing-zeros`, `poly+`, `poly*`, `poly-zero?`.

### Ejercicio 2 — Números Complejos

| Parte | Puntaje | Descripción |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| (a) | 6 Pt | Extensión de `Expr` con `real`, `imaginary`, `idc`, `withc` |
| (b) | 6 Pt | `parser` con soporte para números reales, imaginarios, `+`, `-`, `if0`, `with` secuencial |
| (c) | 6 Pt | `from-CValue`, `cmplx+`, `cmplx-`, `cmplx0?` |
| (d) | 10 Pt | `subst` con manejo de shadowing (variables oscurecidas no se sustituyen) |
| (e) | 10 Pt | `interp` que reduce a `CValue` usando ambiente interno; error si variable libre |

### Supuestos adoptados:
- El `with` en P2 usa definiciones secuenciales (como `let*` en Scheme)
- `subst` para `withc` no sustituye dentro del ámbito si la variable está ligada
- `interp` implementa evaluación con ambiente (no substitución) para manejar correctamente variables ligadas y definiciones secuenciales
- Los números imaginarios usan sintaxis `(n i)` donde `i` es el símbolo `i`

### Cómo ejecutar:
```bash
cd /workspace
racket T2_tests.rkt    # Ejecuta tests (sin output = todos pasan)
```
